text.title: Speech-to-Text — Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns when integrating STT into production.

failure-mode.description: Real-time captioning routed to OpenAI Whisper batch API — observed latency 3-5s breaks UX; users perceive system hung.
failure-mode.repair: Switch to ElevenLabs Scribe (150ms) or Deepgram Nova-2 (200ms) for real-time. Whisper stays for offline batch.

failure-mode.description: Domain-specific vocabulary mistranscribed — medical terms, product codes, names rendered as English-word lookalikes.
failure-mode.detector: WER on labelled clip >15% AND domain has known custom terms AND custom_vocab empty
failure-mode.repair: Provide custom_vocab list to Deepgram or AssemblyAI; expect 20-40% WER reduction on domain terms.

failure-mode.description: No WER eval before adoption — provider picked from blog post; production accuracy is 25% worse than expected.
failure-mode.detector: No reference WER measurement on a 30-min representative clip per language
failure-mode.repair: Run paired bench across 3 providers on 30-min hold-out clip. Pick by measured WER + cost + latency. Document.

failure-mode.description: Hard-coded single provider — OpenAI transcribe degraded during peak hours; product loses voice features until they recover.
failure-mode.repair: Wire fallback to a different provider OR local faster-whisper. Trigger on consecutive 5xx OR timeout > 10s. Test in staging.

failure-mode.description: Diarization required by product but missing from output — speakers blurred into one transcript line; downstream summarisation conflates them.
failure-mode.detector: diarization == false AND product spec demands speaker labels
failure-mode.repair: Switch to a diarization-capable provider (AssemblyAI, Deepgram, Whisper + pyannote pipeline). Update Transcript JSON to include speakers[].
